parts.title: Music Teacher
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] music class that meets the curriculum goals for [grade X], with three activities and a warm-up.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who is shy about singing in music class, with one encouraging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class the basics of [specific music technique, e.g. playing ukulele], with three steps and a simple song.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. music and culture] into a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a [grade X] student's confidence during a music task.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with three steps and a rhythmic warm-up.
Suggest three ways to use everyday objects (e.g. [specific object, e.g. pencils]) as rhythm instruments in a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with one exercise for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing of a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with three elements (e.g. reflection, performance).
Give me five ideas for making a music lesson for a [grade X] class more playful, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. improvisation], and one game for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a [grade X] class after a music lesson on [specific theme, e.g. musical expression], to capture their thoughts.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with a language arts teacher at a [primary school] to connect music to [specific theme, e.g. poetry], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's ability in [specific music technique, e.g. singing], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, songs) for a music lesson for a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. musical composition].
Suggest three ways to adapt a music lesson for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. hearing impairment], with one solution for each.
Create a template for a weekly music plan for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. singing, rhythm, reflection).
Give me five ideas for using minimal equipment in a music lesson for a [grade X] class to create [specific project, e.g. a rhythm composition], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific music genre, e.g. jazz], with one practical exercise for each.
Write a short guide for managing a disruptive music lesson for a [grade X] class, with three steps and one calming strategy.
Give me an example of a music lesson for a [grade X] class that combines music with [specific theme, e.g. history], with three elements.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the art teacher at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. creativity], with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class where they compose a simple melody using [specific tool, e.g. a digital app], with three steps and one objective.
Give me five ways to make a singing exercise more engaging for a [grade X] class, with one fun twist for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to play a rhythm on [specific instrument, e.g. drums], with three steps and a performance element.
Suggest three ways to connect a music lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. sustainability], with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short rhythmic warm-ups for a [grade X] class that develop [specific goal, e.g. coordination], with one description for each.
Give me an example of a routine for ending a music lesson for a [grade X] class with a short concert, with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. GarageBand]) in a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with one task for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection after a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with three questions about their musical experience.
Give me five ideas for integrating movement into a music lesson for a [grade X] class, with a focus on [grade X], and one exercise for each.
Create a list of three short ways to encourage a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific music technique, e.g. reading notation], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with a Social Studies teacher at a [upper secondary school] to connect music to [specific theme, e.g. world cultures], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class a song in another language over one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple songs for a [grade X] class that strengthen [specific goal, e.g. group cohesion], with a short rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to manage a [grade X] class that loses focus during a music lesson, with one method for each.
Create a template for a term plan for music for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. instruments, singing, theory) and time frames.
Give me five ideas for creating a mini-concert with a [grade X] class with minimal preparation, and one element for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific musical concept, e.g. harmony], with one practical exercise for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. stage fright] in a music lesson, with three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson for a [grade X] class that combines music with [specific theme, e.g. technology], with three elements.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the school choir at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. performances], with one example for each.
